channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Brigadier General Oseni Braimah and Seven Soldiers Buried in Maiduguri After Insurgent Attack
item.description: On Wednesday, the Nigerian Army conducted the burial of Brigadier General Oseni Braimah alongside seven other soldiers who tragically lost their lives in a violent insurgent attack in Borno State. The...

item.title: Supreme Court Restores Honour to Former IAF Pilot After 33 Years
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has directed that a former Indian Air Force pilot, who was dismissed under questionable circumstances in 1993, be given an honourable farewell. The pilot, now in his seventi...

item.title: Kenya police arrest four suspects over murder of British businessman
item.description: Police in Kenya have arrested four men linked to the murder of Campbell Scott, a 58-year-old British businessman, whose body was discovered in a sack of pineapples in Makongo Forest, about 60 miles fr...

item.title: RCB defeat Lucknow Super Giants to top IPL 2026 table, Virat Kohli injury concern and Rishabh Pant’s fitness worry
item.description: In the 23rd match of IPL 2026, Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Lucknow Super Giants by five wickets at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru, jumping to the top of the points table. RCB first restri...

item.title: Young Nairobi Woman Shares Challenges That Led to the End of Her Marriage
item.description: Irene Njeri Wangui from Nairobi got married at just 18 years old after moving to Nakuru for work. She met an older man, 15 years her senior, who promised love, stability, and a formal marriage, includ...

item.title: NCDMB and Seplat prepare for launch of Gas Development Centre of Excellence at DELSU
item.description: Key staff from the Nigerian Content Development and Monitoring Board (NCDMB) join hands with Seplat Energy Plc and Delta State University (DELSU) management to put finishing touches on the Centre of E...

item.title: Report reveals major regional inequality in accidental deaths across the UK linked to deprivation
item.description: A new report by the Royal Society for the Prevention of Accidents (RoSPA) has found that people living in the north of England are twice as likely to die in accidents as those living in London. Accord...

item.title: APC Chairman Says Tinubu Allows Party to Operate Independently
item.description: Nentanwe Yilwatda, di national chairman of di All Progressives Congress (APC), don clarify say President Bola Tinubu no dey interfere with di party's administration. During one media interaction, Yilw...

item.title: EFF acknowledges Malema’s firearm case affects local election preparations
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) have acknowledged that the ongoing firearm case against their leader, Julius Malema, could hinder their campaign preparations for the upcoming local government elec...

item.title: Israel and Lebanon engage in talks to potentially end hostilities amid regional tensions
item.description: On April 15, 2026, Israel’s cabinet convened to discuss a potential ceasefire in Lebanon, following over six weeks of conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah. Lebanese officials indicated that an announce...

item.title: EU Orders Meta to Restore Third-Party AI Access on WhatsApp After Fee Dispute
item.description: The European Commission has announced plans to require Meta Platforms to restore access for third-party AI assistants on its WhatsApp messaging platform. This decision comes after Meta imposed a fee f...

item.title: Remulla: Atong Ang Spotted in Calabarzon, Still Evading Arrest
item.description: Interior and Local Government Secretary Jonvic Remulla confirmed that wanted sabungero suspect, Atong Ang, was spotted in Calabarzon (Region 4-A) two weeks ago. According to Remulla, Ang is constantly...

item.title: Arsenal Secure Champions League Semi-Final Spot After Goalless Draw with Sporting
item.description: Arsenal don manage enter Champions League semi-finals after dem hold Sporting Lisbon to zero-zero draw for Emirates Stadium, which make dem win 1-0 on aggregate. Mikel Arteta side no too play well, bu...

item.title: RBA Warns of Economic Downturn as Unemployment Rate Remains Flat
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has issued a stark warning, suggesting the Australian economy is facing a period of reduced prosperity. The assessment comes as the unemployment rate remained stead...

item.title: Australia confronts complex global threats as Geelong refinery fire is extinguished
item.description: Australia is navigating its most complex and challenging geopolitical environment since the Second World War, according to Defence Minister Richard Marles. Speaking at the National Press Club in Canbe...

item.title: Ontario Expands Alcohol Consumption to Picnic Areas and Beaches in Provincial Parks
item.description: Ontario's Progressive Conservative government, led by Premier Doug Ford, has announced that adults aged 19 and over will soon be allowed to consume alcohol in most areas of provincial parks, extending...

item.title: Aliko Dangote joins global leaders on TIME100 2026 list for business and philanthropy
item.description: Africa big man for industry, Aliko Dangote, don once again make the TIME100 list of the world’s most influential people for 2026. The list wey TIME Magazine drop on April 15, get big names from politi...

item.title: UNDP and Finland support 14 Tanzanian green enterprises under the GreenCatalyst programme
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in Tanzania, through its FUNGUO Innovation Programme, has named 14 Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to benefit from the Finland-backed Green...

item.title: X-energy, backed by Amazon, sets IPO target amid growing nuclear power interest
item.description: Nuclear energy startup X-energy, supported by Amazon, has initiated its investor roadshow ahead of its initial public offering (IPO), targeting a price range of $16 to $19 per share. If successful at ...

item.title: North Vancouver council blocks chlorine plant expansion over safety concerns
item.description: The District of North Vancouver council voted 4-3 to reject Chemtrade's proposal to expand its chlorine production facility, which has been operating since 1957 and supplies over 40% of Canada's drink...
